verses.text: Bildad, with nothing new to say, can only extol God's dominion, the fear He commands, and His countless armies of light. He repeats grand generalities. When arguments fail, men fall back on vague majesty to avoid the real question.

Bildad, with nothing new to say, can only extol God's dominion, the fear He commands, and His countless armies of light. He repeats grand generalities. When arguments fail, men fall back on vague majesty to avoid the real question.

Bildad, with nothing new to say, can only extol God's dominion, the fear He commands, and His countless armies of light. He repeats grand generalities. When arguments fail, men fall back on vague majesty to avoid the real question.

'How then can man be justified with God?' he asks; before the pure heavens, man is but a worm. The truth is real and humbling. Yet Bildad uses it only to crush Job, never asking how a sinner might actually be made just — the question the gospel answers.
